_comment: Connector-specific edits a cli-printing-press reprint or onboard --resume must NOT clobber. check_handfixes.py asserts every marker below is still present (and banned anti-patterns absent) and FAILS CI if a reprint dropped one. This connector was minted from a HAND-AUTHORED spec (no public OpenAPI); see project_connectwise_control_needs_creds_block.

handfixes.summary: manifest.json name/entry_point/command use the clean `connectwise-control-mcp`, not the press `-pp-mcp`
handfixes.why: onboard re-copies the SOURCE manifest each --resume; the press source keeps `-pp-mcp`. The repo ships `connectwise-control-mcp`. See project_onboard_manifest_deleak_and_envvar.

handfixes.summary: manifest.json carries a real product description, not the verbose authored-spec info.description ('Unofficial OpenAPI 3 description ...')
handfixes.why: The hand-authored OpenAPI's info.description (a long unofficial/wire-format note) propagated to the README hero + plugin/marketplace/server.json. Authored a real one-liner in the SOURCE manifest. A fresh mint re-emits the spec description.

handfixes.summary: Per-instance CONNECTWISE_CONTROL_BASE_URL is required in manifest + server.json; BASE_URL and USERNAME are non-secret, PASSWORD secret (HTTP Basic)
handfixes.why: ScreenConnect is appliance/instance-hosted - config.go reads CONNECTWISE_CONTROL_BASE_URL but the press manifest shipped only USERNAME+PASSWORD (the per-instance BASE_URL-omission pattern). Auth is HTTP Basic (USERNAME + PASSWORD); a URL and a username are not secrets, so server.json must mark BASE_URL + USERNAME isSecret:false. A fresh mint drops BASE_URL and marks all three secret. See project_veeam_onboard_traps.

handfixes.summary: Governance has a 'Session and host control' human-in-the-loop tier; gen_governance DROPPED sessions run-command (remote code execution on a guest)
handfixes.why: `sessions run-command` runs an arbitrary command on a customer's guest machine (RCE) - the single highest-risk command - and gen_governance left it out of the tier table entirely (the 'command'-named-write blind spot, same class as connectwise-automate's command-execute). Hand-added a Session-and-host-control tier covering run-command + add-event-to, an Access-grant tier (get-access-token), and Admin (save-user/delete-user). A full reprint re-runs the classifier and reverts. See project_connectwise_automate_onboard_traps + feedback_secret_read_commands_not_read_tier.

handfixes.summary: SKILL.md Auth Setup documents HTTP Basic with all three env vars (BASE_URL + USERNAME + PASSWORD), not the generic single-token `auth set-token` model
handfixes.why: The press generated a generic 'auth setup -> get a key -> set USERNAME=<your-key>' section that omitted PASSWORD + BASE_URL and implied a single token - a user following only it cannot authenticate (HTTP Basic needs username+password+host). Adversarial review caught it (P1). A re-vendor re-emits the generic single-token section.

handfixes.summary: page.json command examples use the stripped `connectwise-control-cli`, never `connectwise-control-pp-cli`
handfixes.why: check_cli_claims is blind to the binary-name prefix, so a `-pp-cli` example would pass the gate but fail for users and ship into the published docs page + llms. See project_veeam_onboard_traps.

handfixes.summary: SKILL.md + guide.md stay free of Printing Press / npx / go-install / openclaw-install-block leaks and the verbose .ashx spec-description intro
handfixes.why: onboard only de-leaks the SKILL.md description. The body H1/prereqs/MCP-install, the openclaw frontmatter `install:` block (printing-press-library go module), guide.md's install section, and the raw authored-spec info.description (with .ashx/christaylorcodes wire-format jargon) all survive a re-vendor. See project_onboard_skillmd_guide_deleak.

handfixes.summary: delete-user / add-event-to / get-info carry NO `create` Cobra alias; the press auto-aliases the alphabetically-first POST command in each resource group to `create`
handfixes.why: The press assigns `Aliases: []string{"create"}` to the first POST command per resource group. Here that lands on `security delete-user` (so `security create` would DELETE an instance user), `sessions add-event-to` (queues control events like Wake-on-LAN), and `audit get-info` - none of which create anything; the delete-user case is a catastrophic footgun. check_cli_claims is blind to Cobra aliases (project_onboard_cobra_alias_cli_claims_blindspot) so the gate passed; CodeRabbit flagged it on PR #99. A reprint re-adds the alias - remove it from all three command files. Systemic press bug; a retro should teach the press not to alias destructive/non-create POSTs to `create`.
